explanation: The property states that IF 'not Eclipse is equal to 1' is true, THEN 'sun angle is less than 45' must always be true. This translates to a global implication. Template 10, G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), directly captures this meaning by setting bool_exp2 to 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' and bool_exp3 to 'sun_angle_lessThan_45'.

The sentence describes a universal implication: 'If A, then B' which translates directly to a global implication LTL formula. Template 30, 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', perfectly captures this. 'bool_exp1' is assigned to 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' representing the condition, and 'bool_exp3' is assigned to 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' representing the consequence.
The property states that globally, if the value of 'not_Eclipse' is equal to 1, then the value of 'sun_angle' must be less than 45 at that same instant.

The natural language statement "If A, then B" implies a global implication in LTL. This means that at every moment in time, if the condition 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' is met, then 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' must also be true. Template 30, (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)), perfectly captures this universally quantified implication.

The property states that globally, if the condition 'not_Eclipse is equal to 1' is met, then 'sun_angle is less than 45' must also be true at that same moment. This is a global implication, directly captured by the G (Globally) operator combined with an implication (->).
The property states that globally, if 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' is true, then 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' must also be true. This directly translates the 'If P then Q' structure as a global implication.

The property states that globally (G), if the condition 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' (not Eclipse is equal to 1) holds true, then the condition 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' (sun angle is less than 45) must also hold true simultaneously. This captures the 'If A then B' relationship and the 'shall' implying it must always be the case.

The property states that at all times (G - Globally), if the condition 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' (bool_exp1) is true, then the condition 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' (bool_exp3) must also be true. This captures the 'If A, then B' causal relationship which must hold continuously.

The natural language statement describes a global implication: whenever 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' is true, it must always be the case that 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' is also true. This is perfectly captured by the LTL template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', where 'bool_exp1' is the condition and 'bool_exp3' is the consequent.

The property states that globally, if 'not_Eclipse_equalTo_1' is true at any point, then 'sun_angle_lessThan_45' must also be true at that same point in time. This directly translates the conditional 'If A then B' into an LTL implication within a global scope.
